title: Epic Spec (declarative structural SSOT)
description: Canonical structural specification for an Epic and its children (Features, Stories, Tasks), consumed by the epic-spec-reconciler (Epic #1182). Lives on disk as .agents/epics/<epic-id>.yaml. The reconciler diffs this spec against live GitHub state and applies structural mutations (Create/Update/Close/Relink) behind a dry-run default. Slugs are stable, GH-incarnation-independent identifiers used for intra-spec dependency edges (dependsOn) and the spec ↔ GH-issue mapping persisted in the sibling .agents/epics/<epic-id>.state.json. This schema describes the structural shape only; agent-execution state (agent::* labels) is intentionally not modelled — that surface belongs to the wave-runner, not the reconciler.

$schema.description: Relative or absolute reference to this schema file. Allowed at the top level so YAML editors with $schema-aware autocomplete (e.g. the Red Hat YAML extension) can resolve the schema from the spec file itself.

epic.description: The Epic this spec describes. Carries the GH issue number, human title, and structural labels (type::epic plus any project-specific structural tags). Execution-state labels (agent::*) MUST NOT appear here.

features.description: Ordered list of Features under this Epic. Order is preserved for human readability but is not load-bearing — wave ordering is driven by stories[].wave + dependsOn, not by feature position.

gates.description: Optional references to baseline + gate-config slugs resolved by the baseline subsystem. Carried in the spec so structural changes can be reviewed alongside the quality-gate config that will run against them.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case identifier used for intra-spec dependency edges (dependsOn) and the spec ↔ GH-issue mapping. Slugs must be unique within their scope (features within an epic, stories within a feature, tasks within a story). Slugs are not GH issue numbers — they survive Epic re-incarnations and ticket renumbering. Pattern: lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, must start with [a-z0-9].

label.description: A GitHub label name. Structural labels only (e.g. type::epic, type::feature, type::story, type::task, persona::*, area::*). Execution-state labels in the AGENT_LABELS allow-list (agent::*) MUST NOT appear in the spec — they are owned by the wave-runner and the reconciler refuses to write them.

labels.description: Structural labels attached to this entity. The reconciler enforces an allow-list (STRUCTURAL_LABELS) and rejects any plan that attempts to update AGENT_LABELS via the structural path.

epic.description: Top-level Epic descriptor. Unlike Features/Stories/Tasks (which are slug-keyed and may not exist in GH yet), the Epic always has a real GH issue number — the spec lives at .agents/epics/<id>.yaml and that <id> is the same number used here.

id.description: GitHub issue number for the Epic. Authoritative — the spec file's filename derives from this value (.agents/epics/<id>.yaml) and the sibling state file (<id>.state.json) is keyed against it.

title.description: Human-readable Epic title. Reconciler updates the GH issue title to match this value on every apply (subject to mayUpdate gating).

body.description: Optional Epic body. When present, the reconciler updates the GH issue body to match. When omitted, the GH issue body is left untouched (allowing operator-curated bodies to coexist with structural reconciliation). Multi-line markdown is fully supported.

labels.description: Structural labels attached to the Epic (e.g. type::epic plus any project-specific structural tags). agent::* labels are forbidden — they are owned by the wave-runner.

feature.description: A Feature groups Stories under a common product/architectural theme. Features themselves are GitHub issues (type::feature) when materialised by the reconciler — they carry no execution state, only structure.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this Feature. Must be unique within the parent Epic's features[] array.

body.description: Optional Feature body. Same semantics as epic.body — written by the reconciler when present, left untouched when omitted.

labels.description: Structural labels attached to this Feature (e.g. type::feature plus any project-specific structural tags).

stories.description: Ordered list of Stories under this Feature. Order is for readability; execution order is driven by stories[].wave + dependsOn.

story.description: A Story is the unit of agent execution — exactly one Story branch (story-<id>) and exactly one Story sub-agent per Story per wave. Stories declare their wave assignment and inter-Story dependencies via slug references; the wave-runner consumes these to schedule the parallel fan-out.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this Story. Must be unique within the parent Feature's stories[] array, and is the value referenced by sibling Stories' dependsOn arrays.

title.description: Human-readable Story title. Becomes the GH issue title.

body.description: Optional Story body. Written by the reconciler when present; left untouched when omitted. Used to carry Story-level acceptance criteria / context that the decomposer authored.

wave.description: Zero-indexed wave number. The wave-runner groups Stories with the same wave value into a single parallel batch and waits for every Story in wave N to close before dispatching wave N+1. Wave 0 typically contains Stories with no inbound dependsOn edges.

dependsOn.description: Slugs of Stories (within this Epic) that must close before this Story may dispatch. Slugs reference stories[].slug values — not GH issue numbers — so the spec survives GH re-incarnations. The reconciler resolves slugs to issue numbers via the sibling state file when writing GH-side `blocked by` references.

items.description: Slug of a sibling Story that must close before this Story may dispatch.

labels.description: Structural labels attached to this Story (e.g. type::story, persona::*). agent::* labels are forbidden — they are owned by the wave-runner at dispatch time.

tasks.description: Ordered list of Tasks under this Story. The Story sub-agent walks tasks[] in order during /story-execute; each Task produces exactly one commit on the Story branch via task-commit.js.

task.description: A Task is the smallest unit of agent work — exactly one commit on the parent Story's branch. Tasks have no wave/dependsOn surface: ordering within a Story is purely positional (the order they appear in stories[].tasks), and cross-Task dependencies are expressed implicitly via that ordering. Cross-Story or cross-Wave dependencies belong on the parent Story's dependsOn, not on the Task.

slug.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this Task. Must be unique within the parent Story's tasks[] array.

title.description: Human-readable Task title. Becomes the GH issue title and the conventional-commit subject prefix.

body.description: Optional Task body. Written by the reconciler when present; left untouched when omitted. Typically carries the Task's Goal/Changes/Acceptance/Verify sections from the decomposer.

labels.description: Structural labels attached to this Task (e.g. type::task, persona::*). agent::* labels are forbidden — they are owned by the wave-runner.

gates.description: Optional references to baseline + gate-config slugs. Carried in the spec so structural changes can be reviewed alongside the quality-gate config that will run against them. Resolved to baseline-service inputs by the existing baseline subsystem; opaque to the reconciler itself.

baseline.description: Slug naming the baseline ratchet snapshot this Epic targets (e.g. ratchet-baseline-2026-05-12). The baseline subsystem resolves this to concrete lint/mi/crap baseline files at close-validation time.

config.description: Slug naming the gate-config version this Epic uses (e.g. gate-config-v6). Allows different Epics to opt into different gate configurations during long-lived migrations.
